description: This figure presents electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) results of Fe–30Mn–8Al–C low-density steels with 0, 1, 3 wt% Cu addition (denoted 0 Cu, 1 Cu, 3 Cu respectively) tested in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution. Subplot (a) is the Nyquist diagram, plotting imaginary impedance Zim against real impedance Zre, with an inset equivalent circuit (solution resistance Rs in series with a parallel combination of charge transfer resistance Rct and constant phase element CPE) used for data fitting. Subplot (b) is the Bode diagram, showing impedance modulus |Z| and negative phase angle as functions of frequency from 1E-3 Hz to 1E6 Hz. All experimental data points are well matched by fitting curves. The diameter of the capacitive semicircle in the Nyquist plot decreases with increasing Cu content: 0 Cu has the largest semicircle (highest charge transfer resistance), followed by 1 Cu and 3 Cu. The Bode plot also shows decreasing |Z| and peak phase angle with higher Cu content. These results confirm that Cu alloying deteriorates the corrosion resistance of the studied low-density steel, and higher Cu content leads to worse corrosion performance.
